mechanism_narrative: A sustained high federal funds rate indicates restrictive monetary policy, increasing the cost of capital and reducing corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above 4%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, or the signal's predictive power is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth slows significantly while the federal funds rate remains elevated, it indicates a severe demand contraction exacerbated by tight monetary conditions, which will lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate revenues decline.
regime_hint: consumer spending slowdown with tight monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when RSAFS growth is negative and FEDFUNDS is above 4%, or the interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS shows no significant negative correlation with forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, increasing industrial production indicates a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity, leading to improved corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession recovery periods, INDPRO growth shows no significant positive correlation with SPX 5d returns, or SPX returns are negative despite strong INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation, exceeding 3% year-over-year, signals an environment where the central bank will likely maintain or increase interest rates, thereby dampening economic growth and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month change exceeds 3%, or the correlation between high inflation and negative returns is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real GDP indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of periods where GDPC1 growth is positive and above its historical average, or the Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust job creation, indicated by strong payroll employment growth, combined with healthy housing starts, signals strong consumer confidence and investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as economic activity accelerates.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS growth and HOUST are above their 6-month moving averages, SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 40% of instances, or the interaction term shows no significant positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales, indicated by a 3-month annualized growth rate below -5%, signals a significant decline in consumer demand, which will immediately translate into negative S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 30% of instances where RSAFS 3-month annualized growth falls below -5%, or the signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance.
